summary: Fair audience tape of a spring ’77 show with standout Samson, Mississippi Half‑Step and a soaring Comes a Time despite pitch glitches.
review: The performance shines with Jerry’s fluid solos, Bob’s fiery rhythm work and Keith’s hot keyboards, delivering highlights like Samson & Delilah, Mississippi Half‑Step, Tennessee Jed and an inspired Comes a Time. The audience recording suffers from noticeable pitch wobble, low‑end clipping and occasional distortion, but the music still comes through for most listeners.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (70% weight) while noting significant recording flaws (30% weight), resulting in a solid but not stellar overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Pitch wobble throughout (+/-1‑3%), low‑end clipping, occasional tape‑melt distortion and a weak house mix; crowd chatter present but overall listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as hot and fluid, while the second set drops a bit in energy for some listeners but still contains several high‑points such as Samson & Delilah and the extended Playing > Not Fade Away jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry’s soaring solos, his use of envelope filters and a well‑tuned vocal on Comes a Time.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob (referred to as Bobby) receives repeated praise for his rhythm work on Tennessee Jed, Mississippi Half-Step and Samson & Delilah.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards are highlighted as “hot” with both Moog and piano clearly audible; many call the keyboard work a highlight of the night.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers are described as tight and driving throughout the set.
